fields.label: Search the docs
fields.placeholder: Search the docs — APIs, SDKs, CRM cards, Breeze agent tools

copy.note: Reference, guides, and API changelogs across the developer platform.

rationale: Single hero section per the brief. Chose the hero-search-first genre archetype because the brief names the search field as the hero's primary control — a compact working masthead that routes developers to answers fast, not a persuasion band. The excluded code/terminal skeleton is honored (no mono tier / code asset in HubSpot v2). Standalone-stack alignment context: I OMIT section.alignment so the brand's header grammar resolves the anchor (fixing the prior alignment-resolution failure where a bare 'left' anchor named no counterweight — a compact standalone masthead has no natural counterweight slot, so omission + brand resolution is the honest fix). Surface is inverse (deep teal) as the opening bookend that carries the page's single accent on the display-title's terminal period. Heading is sentence-case, weight ≤500 (never-heavy-headings). The search form binds the brief's exact placeholder vocabulary and a working submit affordance; popular entry points render as quiet arrow links (CRM API / UI extensions / OAuth / webhooks) hugging their labels. No photo, so no text-on-media treatment is needed.
